Hey, welcome back and it's time to go into the module on script building which is the most misunderstood part of the straight line system. So here's the deal. People have this tendency to think that scripts in some way might be unethical or they'll make them sound wooden and they'll reduce their closing rate. But the truth is that when you've mastered the art of using the script the right way, which means creating and knowing how to deliver a straight line script, not only will it increase your closing rate dramatically, but it will also help you stay on the straight line. It'll help make sure you stay ethical. You don't go off into Pluto and say crazy stuff, right, and get your company in trouble, but it just makes you more effective on every single level. Now for those of you who read the book The Way of the Wolf, you haven't read it, you should buy my book, right? But in all seriousness here, I start the chapter on scripting by talking about mastering the art of charisma. And I say, you know, it's kind of a weird way to talk about scripting, say, what does charisma have to do with scripting? But here's the deal. So charisma, let me explain it to you, is basically the art of having that magnetic pull, be able to attract people to you when you're walking or say, wow, you know, that person has charisma. It's a certain sub-ment, you know, intangible quality where people simply just want to be with you in your magnetic zone and you can't quite put your finger on it. But in fact, you actually can. You see, charisma is an entirely learnable skill. It's made up of three different components. And before I tell them to you, let me first ask you a question. Do you think you're one of those lucky people who's blessed with, you know, natural boat loads of charisma? Yes or no? You think you really, really have that magnetic pull? Give me the answer right now. Yes or no? Awesome. Well, you're one of the lucky people that has that natural charisma like, you know, Bill Clinton is the perfect example. You know, President Clinton, you know, he was, you know, that sort of guy that kind of lied to us and did things in the oval office. He probably shouldn't be doing like, I did not have sex, sure, Bill, right? And when Bill Clinton kind of lied, he got caught, he got impeached by the House representatives, right? And I loved him anyway, because here's the deal. When you got into Bill Clinton's magnetic zone and he shook your hand, you looked him in the eyes, you just got this feeling like, wow, he connects with me. He understands me. He feels my pain. And basically that is the essence of which all rapport is built. He understands me, he gets me and he feels my pain. And Bill Clinton had that in just massive abundance. He kind of got away with a lot of stuff that other people couldn't get away with, right? Flipside, you know, George Bush, the second Bush, a George H.W. Bush, right? He didn't have that charisma and Trump has it on some level, but on other levels he also repels people. So it's not quite as clear. But Bill Clinton, he just had that quality, that impangible quality where people just liked him because you felt like he truly understood you. All right, well, that's fine, by the way, because here's the good news. Charisma is actually an entirely learnable skill. You know, we see certain people that have Charisma, like perfect example, President Bill Clinton, right? I mean, he did some stuff in the Oval Office that wasn't quite right, you know, I don't know, I mentioned her name, right? But we all know what happened with Bill Clinton. He lied to us. He even got impeached. But yet, he still kind of liked them. It was something about Bill Clinton. And here's what it was. When we got into his magnetic zone, in that instant that he shook your hand, looked you in the eye and said, how are you? You felt like he understood you, he got you. He felt your pain. He understands me. He gets me. He feels my pain. That's the essence of where all rapport is built. And it's the heart of having massive Charisma. So again, good news. You can be taught to have Charisma. And let me show you exactly how. Now Charisma is made up of three distinct attributes. And I'll tell you exactly what they are. Number one, you must have effective tonality, meaning the words that you say. Have the right tonality applied to them at exactly the right moment. When you have that, it sets you up to be interesting for people to understand that you care about them. From the tonality section, you feel their pain. So we have actually specific tonalities with the straight line that apply just to that. So number one, you have to have awesome tonality. Number two, you need appropriate or essentially targeted body language. Meaning that the way you move your body, the way you make eye contact, the way you shake hands, essentially that when someone sees you there, you lay eyes on you. They just say, you know, wow, this person's like an expert. They're sharp. They get me. Same thing, right? And it comes through in that first quarter of a second, right? You have to have effective, targeted body language. So number one is effective tonality. Number two is targeted body language. And number three, the third aspect of Charisma, which would be the most difficult part of all for most people, is not saying stupid shit. See bottom line is you can have all the great tonality in the world. You have terrific body language, targeted body language, but if you open up your mouth to speak and the words just don't make sense, any rapport that you had, any Charisma that you thought you created, flying out the window. And I see this happen all the time with salespeople. In other words, they start off the presentation and they sound pretty good for the first three or four minutes, but then if the sales starts to drag on and they run out of things to say, all of a sudden that stupid SHIT comes pouring out in boatloads of abundance. And as soon as you do that, what happens with the prospect? They say novice alert, flashing in their mind and just like that, all the good will that you created from starting off with those first four seconds and your intelligence gathering. If you start saying stupid SHIT, it's gone and you're back to square one. So again, in this module, what I'm going to show you how to do is how to create, how to practice and then how to really perfect the delivery of these straight line scripts. And just, you know, straight line scripts, it's really a series of scripts that all go into one cohesive presentation that goes from the first word out of your mouth right till the very end, past your looping patterns, till when the customer says, yes, let's do it. And even after that, to getting referrals and creating customers for life. All right, now I want to stop here. I want you to take a very short quiz, an exercise or two, and then come back and meet me in the first lesson of scripting. It's going to be an awesome module. I'll see you soon.
